marketContext.summary: Broad risk appetite is soft enough that downside continuation and failed bounce setups deserve extra attention.

reasons: price is holding below the Bollinger basis and leaning toward the lower band
Bollinger bands are expanding, which currently supports downside continuation more than snap-back risk
technicalNotes: Price is pressing the lower Bollinger band while the bands expand, which keeps downside continuation in play.
The Bollinger basis is sloping down by -0.32%, which keeps short-term trend control with sellers.
Band width is wider than its recent average, which confirms that the current move is active instead of sleepy.
Recent 24h range support is near 2330.13 and resistance is near 2422.79.
shortCandidates.thesis: ETH-USDT-SWAP is underperforming the broader liquid futures basket and still looks vulnerable to downside continuation if the tape does not recover.
shortCandidates.invalidation: Reassess if price rises above 2360.84 or the 6h trend turns back up.

beginnerPlan.partialExitRule: If price reaches TP1 on the way down, consider taking 30-50% off. If it reaches TP2, consider closing most or all of the rest.
beginnerPlan.timingNote: This is designed for a same-day short. If price stops trending lower after the hold window, reduce or close the trade.
beginnerPlan.scaleOutGuide: Take 30% at TP1, 50% at TP2, and trail the last 20% only if the trend still confirms.
beginnerPlan.priceLogic: These levels use recent hourly volatility. The stop is about 1R away, TP1 is near 1.3R, and TP2 is near 2.2R.
beginnerPlan.leverageNote: Conservative in-app heuristic for beginners, not exchange advice.
beginnerPlan.starterNote: For beginners, do not add to a losing futures position.
